meta.title: G11/G12 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW 7 Series G11/G12 engine — B57, B58, N63, N74 V12. Real UK costs, reliability data and common failures. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 7 Series G11/G12 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned G11/G12 engine typically costs £3,200–£15,000 supply only, depending on engine — the B57 diesel sits at £4,500–£7,500, while the N63 V8 runs £6,000–£11,000 and the N74 V12 can exceed £15,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 7 Series G11/G12 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58B30 (740i) — BMW's modular straight-six is widely regarded as one of the most reliable engines BMW has ever produced. It's so good Toyota uses it in the Supra. The B57 diesel (730d/740d) is a close second. Both are proven, durable, and free of the V8's complexity.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 7 Series G11/G12?

acceptedAnswer.text: N63 V8 valve stem seal and cooling system failures (750i), B57 diesel EGR cooler and DPF issues (730d/740d/750d), B58 cooling system and PCV valve issues (740i), and — unrelated to the engine — G11/G12 air suspension system failures (all models).

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 7 Series G11/G12 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends heavily on which engine you choose. The B58 petrol straight-six and B57 diesel straight-six are both reliable and proven. The N63 V8 is a glorious engine but has well-documented issues — budget for valve-stem-seal and cooling system maintenance. The N74 V12 in the M760Li is spectacular but demands a significant maintenance budget. The air suspension system is a common failure point across all models.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 7 Series G11/G12 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N63 V8 (750i, pre-LCI) — it has well-documented valve-stem-seal and cooling system issues that are expensive to fix. The N74 V12 (M760Li) is not an 'avoid' engine — it's an 'understand what you're getting into' engine. If you want the most trouble-free 7 Series, buy a 740i (B58) or 730d (B57).

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my G11/G12 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Blue smoke on startup (N63 valve stem seals), coolant loss or overheating (N63, B58), engine warning lights (EGR/DPF issues), or persistent loss of power are the key warning signs. For the air suspension, listen for a noisy compressor or notice the car sitting unevenly. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 7 Series G11/G12 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on the car's value. Against a well‑kept £30,000 G11/G12, a £6,500 N63 recon is ~22% of the car's value — safe. Against a tired £16,000 example, the same recon brushes our 70% BMW threshold — walk away. Also factor in the condition of the air suspension system — this can be a significant additional cost.

mainEntity.name: How long do BMW 7 Series G11/G12 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58 straight-six can exceed 200,000 miles with proper servicing. The B57 diesel is similarly durable. The N63 V8's lifespan depends on valve-stem-seal and cooling system maintenance — with proper care, it can reach 150,000+ miles. The N74 V12 is the most expensive to maintain but can also reach high mileages with meticulous care.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the G11 and the G12?

acceptedAnswer.text: The G11 is the short-wheelbase 7 Series (standard length), while the G12 is the long-wheelbase version with an additional 140 mm (5.5 inches) of rear legroom. Both share the same platform, engines, and interior design. The G12 is more popular in markets like China and the US, while the G11 is more common in Europe. Most UK buyers choose the G12 for the extra rear space.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 7 Series G11/G12 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; block stamping is visible from below on B47/B57/B58/N63/N74 units. Tell us your variant (725d, 730d, 740d, 750d, 730i, 740i, 740e, 745e, 750i, M760Li, Alpina B7) and year and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted BMW specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.h1: BMW 7 Series G11/G12 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the sixth-generation 7 Series — from the B47 four-cylinder diesel to the N74 V12 that powered the M760Li, and the B58 six-cylinder that redefined the plug-in hybrid range. Real UK replacement costs, honest failure data, and the N63 V8 issues that still haunt early cars. Backed by 256+ real UK G11/G12 enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

engineDatabase.h2: BMW 7 Series G11/G12 Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the sixth-generation 7 Series — from the entry-level four-cylinder diesel to the range-topping V12, including the plug-in hybrid variants.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: All reliability ratings are [PROVISIONAL] — no dedicated Engine Page exists yet for any G11/G12 engine family. Ratings are derived from cross-referencing specialist and owner sources. The B57 diesel and B58 petrol are widely regarded as BMW's most reliable modern engines, with the quad-turbo B57 (750d) carrying additional complexity risk. The N63 V8 has well-documented issues (valve stem seals, cooling system) that persist across generations. The N74 V12 is robust but parts and labour are extremely expensive.

overview.intro: The G11/G12 is the sixth generation of the BMW 7 Series, unveiled on 10 June 2015 and launched in October 2015. It succeeded the F01/F02 7 Series with a carbon-fibre-reinforced plastic (CFRP) core, a lighter aluminium-intensive body, and BMW's first plug-in hybrid powertrain in the 7 Series range. The G11 denotes the short-wheelbase version; G12 denotes the long-wheelbase version. A 2019 Life Cycle Impulse (LCI) brought the largest grille ever fitted to a BMW production car (40% larger than pre-LCI), revised powertrains including the B58 six-cylinder replacing the four-cylinder in the plug-in hybrid, and significant power increases for the V8 models.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW CLAR (Carbon Core) • Predecessor: BMW 7 Series F01/F02 (2008–2015) • Successor: BMW 7 Series G70 (2022–present) • Sibling body styles in this generation: G11 (short-wheelbase saloon), G12 (long-wheelbase saloon)
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The G11/G12 7 Series generated 256 real UK engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED], concentrated on the B57 diesel and N63 V8 petrol units as these luxury flagships exit warranty and owner maintenance cycles begin.

items.quote: The B57 is BMW's modular straight-six diesel and one of the most refined diesel engines ever fitted to a 7 Series. The 730d delivers 265 bhp and 620 Nm of torque; the 740d ups that to 320 bhp and 680 Nm. Both offer effortless performance and genuine 40+ MPG economy. Unlike the quad-turbo 750d, the single and twin-turbo B57s are proven, durable, and free of excessive complexity. It's the diesel that defines this generation.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want flagship luxury with diesel economy and proven reliability.

items.quote: The B58 is BMW's modular straight-six and arguably the best engine ever fitted to a 7 Series — 326 bhp pre-LCI, rising to 340 bhp post-LCI. It's smooth, responsive, and so reliable that Toyota uses it in the Supra. The 740i is the sweet spot in the petrol range — six-cylinder refinement, V8-rivalling performance, and none of the N63's complexity or the V12's running costs. It's the 7 Series engine for people who actually drive their cars.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the best all-round petrol 7 Series without the V8's maintenance bills.

items.quote: The N63 V8 is a glorious engine — 449 bhp, 650 Nm, and a soundtrack that defines the 7 Series experience. But it carries BMW's 'hot-vee' design, where the turbos sit inside the V of the engine, generating significant heat. Valve stem seals harden and cause blue smoke on startup; cooling system components fail prematurely; and the alternator bracket gasket is prone to oil leaks. The post-LCI N63B44TÜ3 (523 bhp) is improved but still carries more risk than the B58.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want V8 power and are prepared for V8 maintenance costs.

items.quote: The N74 V12 is BMW's final production V12 — 609 bhp, 800 Nm, and a straight-line performance that's genuinely staggering (0-60 in 3.6 seconds). It's also one of the most expensive engines to maintain: parts are rare, labour is specialist-only, and the engine bay is tightly packed. A full V12 replacement can exceed £15,000. Only 5,000+ M760Li examples were built, making them rare but costly to own.
items.whoItsFor: collectors who want BMW's final V12 and have the budget to maintain it.

items.quote: The 730d delivers 265 bhp, 620 Nm, and genuine 45+ MPG economy in a full-size luxury saloon. It's the entry point to the diesel range but punches above its weight — six-cylinder refinement, proven B57 durability, and none of the quad-turbo complexity of the 750d. It's the sensible choice that still delivers the 7 Series experience.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want flagship luxury with the lowest running costs.

items.quote: The 745e replaced the 740e's four-cylinder with a B58 six-cylinder, delivering 394 bhp system output and an electric range of up to 36 miles. It's often overlooked in favour of the V8s and diesels, but it's the most advanced powertrain in the range — six-cylinder refinement, electric-only city driving, and genuine performance. It's the 7 Series for buyers who want future-proof luxury.
items.whoItsFor: buyers who want the most technologically advanced 7 Series and can charge at home.

economicsRule.title: The G11/G12 rule
economicsRule.text: A £6,000–£11,000 N63 recon against a £15,000–£40,000 7 Series sits at 15–73% of the car's value. On a sub-£18,000 high-mileage example, that brushes our 70% BMW threshold — we'll honestly tell you to walk away. Against a well-kept £30,000+ example, the same recon drops to a routine 20–37% — a repair decision, not a financial one. Value the car first, and always check service history on V8 and V12 cars.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G11/G12 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: 730d (B57) — proven six-cylinder diesel, strong economy, no V8 complexity.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G11/G12 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: M760Li (N74 V12) — V12 running costs, rare parts, specialist-only maintenance.

keyTakeaways.question: Which G11/G12 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: 740i (B58) — the best petrol engine in the range, strong residuals, the one enthusiasts want.

problems.title: N63 V8 Valve Stem Seal & Cooling System Failure
problems.rootCause: The N63's 'hot-vee' design — turbos positioned inside the V of the engine — generates significant heat that accelerates wear on valve stem seals. The seals harden and lose their sealing ability, allowing oil to seep past and into the combustion chamber. The classic symptom is a puff of blue smoke on cold startup. The cooling system (water pump, thermostat, expansion tank) also fails prematurely due to the extreme under-bonnet temperatures. Post-LCI N63B44TÜ3 (523 bhp) is improved but not immune.
problems.recommendation: If your N63 V8 smokes on startup, budget for valve-stem-seal replacement immediately — a £1,500–£3,000 repair is dramatically cheaper than the £6,000+ replacement that follows if the catalytic converters are destroyed or the engine overheats. Regular oil changes (5,000-mile intervals) with high-quality oil are essential.

cta.label: Compare N63 engine prices for your G11/G12 750i →

problems.title: B57 Diesel EGR Cooler & DPF Issues
problems.rootCause: The B57 diesel's EGR cooler can develop leaks or become clogged with soot, leading to coolant loss, poor running, and engine warning lights. The DPF (diesel particulate filter) can clog with repeated short journeys, requiring forced regeneration or replacement. The 750d's quad-turbo setup adds additional complexity and potential failure points.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of reduced performance or an engine warning light, have the EGR and DPF systems inspected. A £350–£700 service is dramatically cheaper than the £1,200+ replacement that follows a cracked cooler or blocked DPF. Regular long motorway runs help prevent DPF issues.

cta.label: Compare B57 engine prices for your G11/G12 730d/740d →

problems.title: B58 Cooling System & PCV Valve Issues
problems.rootCause: The B58 is widely regarded as BMW's most reliable modern engine, but it's not without issues. The plastic cooling system components (water pump, thermostat housing, coolant pipes) can become brittle and crack. The PCV (positive crankcase ventilation) valve can fail, causing whistling noises and rough running. These are well-documented B58 weaknesses but are far less catastrophic than N63 issues.
problems.recommendation: Check coolant levels regularly and listen for whistling noises from the engine bay. A £250–£550 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £3,500+ replacement that follows an overheat or PCV failure.

cta.label: Compare B58 engine prices for your G11/G12 740i →

problems.title: G11/G12 Air Suspension System Failure
problems.affectedModels: All G11/G12 variants, particularly high-mileage examples.
problems.rootCause: Not an engine failure, but the single most common non-engine complaint on the G11/G12 7 Series. The air suspension system — air springs, compressor, valve block, and sensors — is prone to failure. The compressor can overheat and fail; air springs can develop leaks; and the system can become unlevel. Replacement costs are significant.
problems.recommendation: Have the air suspension system inspected at every service — a £200–£500 preventative check is dramatically cheaper than the £1,500+ replacement that follows if the compressor fails or the car is left sitting on its bump stops.

cta.label: Compare G11/G12 air suspension repair quotes →

The 725d was produced 2016–2019 only; the 750d (quad-turbo) was produced 2017–2020 only. M760Li xDrive is the sole V12 variant. Alpina B7 is a separate variant based on the G12 platform.
